title: Schematic & Loadout Management

use_cases.name: Browse Schematics
use_cases.description: Player views grid of all saved schematics with thumbnails (SchematicImage). Filtered by search text and selected tags.

use_cases.name: Search Schematics
use_cases.description: Player types in searchField; ignoreSymbols regex strips punctuation; rebuildPane filters schematics by name match.

use_cases.name: Filter Schematics by Tags
use_cases.description: Player toggles tag buttons; selectedTags Seq updated; rebuildPane re-filters by s.labels.containsAll(selectedTags).

use_cases.name: View Schematic Info
use_cases.description: showInfo(Schematic) opens inner SchematicInfoDialog showing dimensions, tiles count, requirements (ItemSeq), power balance, tags.

use_cases.name: Deploy / Use Schematic
use_cases.description: In-game: clicking schematic calls control.input.useSchematic(s) and hides dialog. Also triggered via keyboard shortcut (Binding.chat) on firstSchematic.

use_cases.name: Import Schematic
use_cases.description: Three paths: (1) clipboard – Schematics.readBase64(clipboardText); (2) file – platform.showFileChooser → Schematics.read(file); (3) Steam Workshop – platform.openWorkshop(). Calls schematics.add(s), checkTags(s), showInfo(s).

use_cases.name: Export Schematic
use_cases.description: Three paths: (1) clipboard – schematics.writeBase64(s); (2) file – platform.export(name, ext, file → Schematics.write(s, file)); (3) Steam Workshop – platform.publish(s).

use_cases.name: Edit Schematic Metadata
use_cases.description: Player edits name and description via text fields. On accept: s.tags.put('name'), s.tags.put('description'), s.save(), rebuildPane.run().

use_cases.name: Delete Schematic
use_cases.description: Trash button shows confirmation; on confirm: schematics.remove(s), rebuildPane.run(). Mod-owned schematics cannot be deleted.

use_cases.name: Manage Tags (Global)
use_cases.description: Player reorders (swap up/down), renames (replaces across all schematics + saves), or deletes tags. Tags persisted via Core.settings.putJson('schematic-tags').

use_cases.name: Add Tag to Schematic
use_cases.description: From schematic tag row: '+' opens addtag dialog listing available tags. Player picks existing or creates new (text tag via showNewTag / icon tag via showNewIconTag). Calls addTag(schem, tag) → s.labels.add + s.save + tagsChanged.

use_cases.name: Remove Tag from Schematic
use_cases.description: Each tag shows cancel button; calls removeTag(schem, tag) → s.labels.remove + s.save + tagsChanged.

use_cases.name: Create Icon Tag
use_cases.description: Inline icon picker (TODO comment to use IconSelectDialog). Iterates accessibleIcons and defaultContentIcons (UnlockableContent with emoji). Selected char added to tags list.

use_cases.name: Configure Item Loadout
use_cases.description: show(capacity, total, stacks, validator, resetter, updater, hider). Player increments/decrements (step logic: 100/200/500/1000 based on current amount), pencil-edits exact value, or clicks Max to fill all items to capacity. On hide: filters stacks with amount>0, runs updater callback.

use_cases.name: Select Launch Schematic
use_cases.description: show(CoreBlock, Sector, Sector, confirm). Displays available loadout schematics via schematics.getLoadouts() filtered by core.size. Player selects schematic; triggers update of total requirements + capacity.

use_cases.name: Configure Launch Resources
use_cases.description: Only if destination.allowLaunchLoadout(). 'Resources' button opens embedded loadout.show() with realItems = sitems minus schematic requirements. On hide: universe.updateLaunchResources(new ItemSeq(out)).

use_cases.name: Auto-Max Launch Resources
use_cases.description: Toggle 'Max Resources' button (Core.settings 'maxresources'). When on: sets each resource to clamp(sitems - schems, 0, capacity). Disabled if loadout editing is active.

use_cases.name: Launch to Sector
use_cases.description: Launch button enabled only when valid (sitems.has(total) or debugSelect). On click: universe.updateLoadout(core, selected), confirm.run(), hide().

use_cases.name: Select Player Color
use_cases.description: show(Cons<Color>). Grid of playerColors (4 per row). On click: cons.get(color), hide(). Highlights current player.color().

use_cases.name: Select Icon
use_cases.description: show(Intc listener). Grid of accessibleIcons (arc Icon codes) + rows per ContentType (UnlockableContent.emojiChar). On selection: consumer.get(charCode), hide(). Optional allowLocked flag controls whether locked content icons appear.

key_relationships: SchematicsDialog composes SchematicInfoDialog (inner class, extends BaseDialog)
SchematicsDialog composes SchematicImage (inner class, extends Image)
SchematicsDialog uses showNewIconTag() as inline icon picker (not yet delegating to IconSelectDialog)
SchematicsDialog depends_on schematics (Schematics service): all(), add(), remove(), getLoadouts(), writeBase64(), getPreview(), hasPreview()
LaunchLoadoutDialog depends_on universe: getLaunchResources(), updateLaunchResources(), getLoadout(), updateLoadout()
SchematicsDialog depends_on platform: showFileChooser(), export(), publish(), openWorkshop(), viewListing()
LoadoutDialog depends_on content.items() for populating all possible items
include_extend_notes: UC4 (View Info) <<include>> UC7 (Export) and UC8 (Edit) — SchematicInfoDialog buttons call showExport/showEdit
UC6 (Import) <<extends>> with Steam Workshop path only when steam==true
UC7 (Export) <<extends>> with Steam Workshop path only when steam==true and !s.hasSteamID()
UC11 (Add Tag) <<include>> UC13 (Create Icon Tag) or text tag creation
UC15 (Select Launch Schematic) <<include>> UC16 (Configure Resources) when destination.allowLaunchLoadout()
UC16 (Configure Launch Resources) <<include>> UC14 (Configure Item Loadout)
important_notes: SchematicsDialog uses rebuildPane/rebuildTags Runnable callbacks for reactive UI updates. Tags are persisted in Core.settings JSON. LaunchLoadoutDialog validates launch by checking sitems.has(total). LoadoutDialog step() function uses tiered increments (100→200→500→1000). IconSelectDialog supports allowLocked constructor param.
